title: Banking Statistics of Private Sector, Iran, June 1957-June 1959 (World Bank, 1960)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Original document is a 1960 scanned World Bank report (data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1960_current_economic_position_and_prospects.pdf) with a poor-quality OCR text layer (garbled digits in places). Every value in this file was verified by rendering the exact source page (Table 14, page 49) to a high-resolution PNG (pdftoppm -r 200) and visually reading the table directly from the image. The image was clean and unambiguous for all numeric values. No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1960_current_economic_position_and_prospects.pdf — Table 14, page 49, source: Compilation by IBRD Mission based on International Financial Statistics. CSV uses a long/tidy section+line_item format to mirror the source's three blocks (billion-rial levels, percent shares, and advances/deposits ratios). AMBIGUITY FLAGGED: the source prints the 'Excess Cash' row label itself in parentheses -- '(Excess Cash)' -- with its three values also parenthesized -- '(1.58)' '(2.66)' '(3.32)'. This project's other tables use parentheses strictly for negative numbers, but here the values grow in step with the Cash row (2.09->4.57) rather than behaving like a deficit, so it more likely reflects a typographic convention marking this as a derived/memo line rather than a true negative. We transcribed the magnitudes as positive (1.58, 2.66, 3.32) and flagged this note rather than silently picking a sign; a future user with fuller context on 1960 IBRD table conventions should double check the source image (page 49) before treating this row as strictly positive or negative. Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era (esp. 1941-1960) data from primary-source PDFs already in data/raw/.
